Loss of C-terminal K from Heavy Chain of MAb/Deletion of K

project.title: Study on the inhibitory effect and mechanism of VOCs of a deep-sea bacteria on Pseudomonas aeruginosa
project.projectDescription: In order to elucidate the potential mechanism of resistance of active substances to PAO1, we performed a 4D Lable Free proteomic analysis to compare the differentially expressed proteins of PAO1 under the stress of active substances.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: In this study, a series of cutting-edge technologies such as protein extraction, enzyme digestion, liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry tandem analysis and bioinformation analysis were organically combined to study the quantitative proteome of samples.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Most of the peptides are distributed in 7-20 amino acids, which conforms to the general rule based on enzymatic hydrolysis and mass spectrometry fragmentation mode. The length distribution of peptides identified by mass spectrometry meets the quality control requirements. Most proteins have coverage below 30%. In a shotgun(also called bottom-up) approach, mass spectrometry preferentially scans peptides with higher abundance. Thus, protein coverage was positively correlated with abundance in the sample. In order to thoroughly understand the functional properties of different proteins, we carried out comprehensive functional annotation of the identified proteins. The Gene Ontology (GO), Protein domain, KEGG pathway, COG/KOG functional classification, and Subcellular localization are annotated in detail.

labPIs.title: Dr
labPIs.affiliation: Key Laboratory of Experimental Marine Biology, Institute of Oceanology, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Qingdao, China

references.referenceLine: Hu Y, Liu G, Sun C, Wu S. Volatile Organic Compounds Produced by a Deep-Sea Bacterium Efficiently Inhibit the Growth of Pseudomonas aeruginosa PAO1. Mar Drugs. 2024 22(5):233
